summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy fall ’93 show, highlighted by a scorching Wharf Rat, an epic Wave → Crazy Fingers sequence and a soaring Brokedown Palace.
review: The recording is praised as one of the best‑sounding archive sources – pure, crisp soundboard quality with Dick’s Picks immediacy. Musically the second set shines, delivering a fiery Wave to the Wind into Crazy Fingers, a ripping Wharf Rat, an explosive The Other One and a heartfelt Brokedown Palace, though some newer songs such as Broken Arrow and Easy Answers fall flat for a few listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments on sound quality and many standout performances outweigh several criticisms of newer material, yielding an overall good but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, pure soundboard with Dick's Picks‑level immediacy; no reported dropouts or mix issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set mixes strong classics with weaker new material; the second set builds relentless momentum, featuring seamless transitions and high‑energy jams that rank among the tour’s best.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his tone, soaring solos and acoustic work; highlighted as a standout throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for exploding into The Other One and co‑leading an exceptional Wave to the Wind performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's keyboard playing described as surprisingly right and consistently great all night.
